summary: Hedetniemi's 1966 conjecture — that $\chi(G \times H) = \min(\chi(G), \chi(H))$ for the tensor product — was disproved by Shitov in 2019 via finite simple graphs $G,H$ with $\chi(G \times H) < \min(\chi(G), \chi(H))$. Subsequent work produced increasingly smaller counterexamples: Zhu gave explicit counterexamples with chromatic number 225, Tardif and others brought the colourability threshold down to $c=14$ and $c=13$, and Wrochna reduced it further to $c=5$. In the $c$-colourability formulation, the conjecture is now known to fail for $c \ge 4$ and to hold for $c \le 3$.

since_posted.title: Counterexamples to Hedetniemi's conjecture
since_posted.claim: Constructs finite simple graphs $G, H$ with $\chi(G \times H) < \min(\chi(G), \chi(H))$, refuting the conjecture in general.

since_posted.title: Relatively small counterexamples to Hedetniemi's conjecture
since_posted.venue: Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B
since_posted.claim: Improves Shitov's refutation by constructing counterexamples with chromatic number 225 (graphs of 10,952 and 33,377 vertices).

since_posted.title: Smaller counterexamples to Hedetniemi's conjecture
since_posted.claim: Pushes the smallest known counterexample down to $c = 5$ via wide colourings and lexicographic products (graphs of 4686 and 30 vertices).

search_queries: Shitov Hedetniemi conjecture counterexample chromatic number tensor product graphs
notes: The Annals page directly verifies Shitov's title, author, pages 663-667 in volume 190, and DOI 10.4007/annals.2019.190.2.6. Zhu's arXiv page verifies the explicit chromatic-number-225 counterexamples, and the ScienceDirect page verifies the JCTB 2021 publication and DOI. The statement about failure for all $c\ge4$ in the $c$-colourability formulation is supported by the 2025 survey search result; it is included only in the summary, not as a primary counterexample citation.
